top_stories.title: Microsoft Patches Entra ID Role Flaw That Enabled Service Principal Takeover
top_stories.summary: Microsoft patched a flaw in the Entra ID "Agent ID Administrator" role that could allow privilege escalation and service principal takeover attacks. Security firm Silverfort discovered the vulnerability, which stemmed from a privileged built-in role introduced to manage AI agent identity lifecycle operations.

top_stories.title: ConsentFix v3 attacks target Azure with automated OAuth abuse
top_stories.summary: A new attack technique called ConsentFix v3 has emerged on hacker forums, targeting Azure environments through automated OAuth abuse. The attack builds on previous OAuth consent phishing methods by adding automation capabilities that increase its scaling potential.

top_stories.title: Cybercrime Groups Using Vishing and SSO Abuse in Rapid SaaS Extortion Attacks
top_stories.summary: Two cybercrime groups, Cordial Spider and Snarky Spider, are conducting rapid SaaS extortion attacks using vishing and SSO abuse to gain unauthorized access with minimal forensic traces. The attacks exploit SSO infrastructure to move laterally within SaaS environments, enabling high-speed data theft and extortion.

top_stories.title: Learning from the Vercel breach: Shadow AI & OAuth sprawl
top_stories.summary: A security analysis examines the Vercel breach as a case study in OAuth sprawl and shadow AI risks, where a compromised third-party OAuth integration led to widespread impact across downstream customers. The incident illustrates how a single unauthorized OAuth app can serve as an attack vector into connected environments.

top_stories.title: Access control with IAM Identity Center session tags
top_stories.summary: AWS IAM Identity Center supports session tags as a mechanism for implementing attribute-based access control (ABAC) across multi-account AWS environments. The feature enables centralized workforce access management by passing user attributes as session tags to enforce fine-grained authorization policies at scale.

news.title: ChatGPT advanced account security adds passkeys and hardware keys
news.summary: OpenAI has introduced Advanced Account Security for ChatGPT and Codex accounts, an opt-in feature that disables password-based login in favor of passkeys or physical hardware security keys. Enrolled accounts also lose email and SMS account recovery options, making authentication fully phishing-resistant.

news.title: Poisoned Ruby Gems and Go Modules Exploit CI Pipelines for Credential Theft
news.summary: A software supply chain attack campaign using malicious Ruby gems and Go modules has been attributed to the GitHub account "BufferZoneCorp," with packages designed to steal credentials and tamper with CI pipelines. The attack leverages sleeper packages to enable credential theft, GitHub Actions manipulation, and SSH persistence.

news.title: IAM Roles Anywhere now enforces VPC endpoint policies for the CreateSession API
news.summary: AWS IAM Roles Anywhere now supports VPC endpoint policies for the CreateSession API, allowing administrators to control access at the network level. This update enables organizations to configure allow or deny rules for CreateSession API calls through VPC endpoints, adding network-based access controls to workload identity management.

news.title: IAM tools help Oracle Red Bull Racing keep pace with strict F1 regulations
news.summary: Oracle Red Bull Racing implemented 1Password's identity technology to improve efficiency in aerodynamics testing procedures, particularly in meeting F1 regulations. The deployment focused on identity management workflows to streamline compliance with strict Formula 1 testing rules.

news.title: Govern your bots carefully or chaos could ensue
news.summary: Gartner warns that AI agent sprawl poses significant governance risks, with Global Fortune 500 enterprises expected to run over 150,000 AI agents by 2028, up from fewer than 15 today. The article addresses the need for proper governance frameworks to manage non-human identities and autonomous agents, a growing concern within identity governance and IGA disciplines.

news.title: Webinar: How to Automate Exposure Validation to Match the Speed of AI Attacks
news.summary: A webinar is being promoted around the threat of AI-automated attacks targeting Active Directory and Domain Admin credentials. The session focuses on autonomous agents capable of mapping AD environments and escalating privileges rapidly, framed around exposure validation workflows.

news.title: Critical cPanel Authentication Vulnerability Identified — Update Your Server Immediately
news.summary: cPanel has released security updates addressing a critical authentication vulnerability affecting all supported versions of cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM). The flaw impacts various authentication paths and could allow attackers to gain unauthorized access to the control panel software.

news.title: Organizing Agents’ memory at scale: Namespace design patterns in AgentCore Memory
news.summary: AWS published a technical post covering namespace design patterns for AgentCore Memory, including IAM-based access control implementation. The article addresses how to structure memory hierarchies and apply IAM policies within the AgentCore agent framework on AWS.

news.title: AWS Transfer Family Terraform module now supports Okta and Microsoft Entra ID integration examples
news.summary: AWS has updated its Transfer Family Terraform module to include end-to-end deployment examples integrating Okta and Microsoft Entra ID as custom identity providers for authentication and access control. The update enables enterprises to use existing Okta or Microsoft Entra ID infrastructure to manage authentication for AWS Transfer Family endpoints.

news.title: Why Secure Data Movement Is the Zero Trust Bottleneck Nobody Talks About
news.summary: A research report called "Cyber360: Defending the Digital Battlespace," based on a survey of 500 security professionals, identifies secure data movement as a bottleneck in Zero Trust program implementation. The article argues that connectivity assumptions in Zero Trust architectures lead to program stalls.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Buchanan admitted to conducting SMS phishing attacks in summer 2022 that breached at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in the theft of tens of millions of dollars in cryptocurrency.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign operated without deploying malware, relying solely on stolen authentication tokens to gain unauthorized access.
